FT LAUDERDALE HEALTH & REHABILITATION CENTER

CCN: 105298 · FORT LAUDERDALE, FL 33308 · Broward County

Overview

Address
2000 EAST COMMERCIAL BLVD, FORT LAUDERDALE, FL 33308
Phone
9547712300
Certified beds

Avg daily residents

For-profit LLC
Provider type

Setting
Urban
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.83 4.02 3.68 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.80 0.70 0.77 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.67 0.89 0.64 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.36 2.42 2.27 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.47 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.11 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.55
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.61
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.41
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
33.3%
annual
RN turnover
38.2%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.42
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.03
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-09-19 7 5 2 32 1 32
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-06-29 11 8 4 52 1 52
Total weighted health score
37.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (26)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

26 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-10-02 2025-12-11
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2025-06-30 2025-07-30
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. D 2024-09-19 2024-10-19
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. D 2024-09-19 2024-10-19
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2024-09-19 2024-10-19
0808 Ensure therapeutic diets are prescribed by the attending physician and may be delegated to a registered or licensed dietitian, to the extent allowed by State law. D 2024-09-19 2024-10-19
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2024-09-19 2024-10-19
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2023-10-04 2023-11-04
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-10-04 2023-11-04
0940 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program for all new and existing staff members. D 2023-10-04 2023-11-04
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-06-29 2023-08-10
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2023-06-29 2023-07-29
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2023-06-29 2023-08-10
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2023-06-29 2023-08-10
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-06-29 2023-07-29
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2023-06-29 2023-07-29
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-06-29 2023-08-10
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2023-06-29 2023-08-10
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2022-02-24 2022-03-26
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2022-02-24 2022-03-26
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2022-02-24 2022-03-26
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2022-02-24 2022-03-26
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2022-02-24 2022-03-26
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. D 2022-02-24 2022-03-26
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. D 2022-02-24 2022-03-26
0808 Ensure therapeutic diets are prescribed by the attending physician and may be delegated to a registered or licensed dietitian, to the extent allowed by State law. D 2022-02-24 2022-03-26
